**Tongling** ([simplified Chinese](/wiki/Simplified_Chinese_characters "Simplified Chinese characters"): [铜陵](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E9%93%9C%E9%99%B5 "wikt:铜陵"); [traditional Chinese](/wiki/Traditional_Chinese_characters "Traditional Chinese characters"): [銅陵](https://en.wiktionary.org/wiki/%E9%8A%85%E9%99%B5 "wikt:銅陵"); [pinyin](/wiki/Pinyin "Pinyin"): *Tónglíng*; [Wade–Giles](/wiki/Wade%E2%80%93Giles "Wade–Giles"): *T'ung-ling*; lit. 'Copper Hillock') is a [prefecture-level city](/wiki/Prefecture-level_city "Prefecture-level city") in southern [Anhui](/wiki/Anhui "Anhui") [province](/wiki/Provinces_of_China "Provinces of China") of [China](/wiki/China "China"). A [river port](/wiki/River_port "River port") along the [Yangtze River](/wiki/Yangtze_River "Yangtze River"), Tongling borders [Wuhu](/wiki/Wuhu_City "Wuhu City") to the east, [Chizhou](/wiki/Chizhou "Chizhou") to the southwest and [Anqing](/wiki/Anqing "Anqing") to the west.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

As of the 2020 census, its population was 1,311,726 inhabitants whom 842,675 lived in the built-up (or metro) area made of 3 urban districts.
At the end of 2024 and the beginning of 2025, the permanent population will be 1.302 million.[[4]](#cite_note-4) The asteroid [12418 Tongling](/wiki/12418_Tongling "12418 Tongling") was named after the city.[[5]](#cite_note-5)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Tongling&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [[icon]](/wiki/File:Wiki_letter_w_cropped.svg) | This section **needs expansion**. You can help by [adding missing information](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Tongling&action=edit&section=). *(September 2024)* |

Tongling has been famous as a beautiful mountain city since its origins in the [Han dynasty](/wiki/Han_dynasty "Han dynasty") over 1500 years ago. Owing to its copper and tin deposits, it was an important center in the past for bronze production. The scale of industrial activity gradually increased through the [Ming](/wiki/Ming_dynasty "Ming dynasty") (1368–1644) and [Qing](/wiki/Qing_Dynasty "Qing Dynasty") (1644–1911) dynasties, the Japanese occupation (1938–45), and especially under the [Communist](/wiki/Chinese_Communist "Chinese Communist") government since 1949. Tongling has a long history and produced many talented people. It is one of the important birthplaces of China's bronze culture. The history of copper mining and smelting has lasted for more than 3,000 years without interruption.[[6]](#cite_note-6) Tongling has been a mining center since the 7th century CE. While it has only been an [industrial city](/wiki/Industrial_city "Industrial city") for the second half of the 20th century.[[7]](#cite_note-7)

## Geography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Tongling&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Geography")]

Tongling is located in southern Anhui on the southern (right) bank of the lower reaches of the Yangtze River, with latitude ranging from 30° 45' 12" to 31° 07' 56" N and longitude ranging from 117° 42' 00" to 118° 10' 06" E;[[8]](#cite_note-8) the maximal north–south extent is 42.5 kilometres (26.4 mi), and the greatest east–west width is 40.6 km (25.2 mi).[[9]](#cite_note-City_intro-9) It borders [Fanchang County](/wiki/Fanchang_County "Fanchang County") to the southeast, [Qingyang County](/wiki/Qingyang_County "Qingyang County") and [Nanling County](/wiki/Nanling_County "Nanling County") to the south, [Guichi District](/wiki/Guichi_District "Guichi District") of [Chizhou](/wiki/Chizhou "Chizhou") to the southwest, and, northwest across the Yangtze, [Wuwei County](/wiki/Wuwei_County "Wuwei County") and [Zongyang County](/wiki/Zongyang_County "Zongyang County").[[10]](#cite_note-weather.com-10)

### Climate

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Tongling&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Climate")]

Tongling City has a northern subtropical humid monsoon climate. The climate in Tongling is warm and humid, with heavy rain in spring and summer, hot summer, dry autumn, mild winter, long frost-free period and distinct four seasons.[[10]](#cite_note-weather.com-10)

## Administration

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Tongling&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Administration")]

The [prefecture-level city](/wiki/Prefecture-level_city "Prefecture-level city") of Tongling administers 4 [county-level divisions](/wiki/Administrative_divisions_of_the_People%27s_Republic_of_China#County_level "Administrative divisions of the People's Republic of China"), including 3 [districts](/wiki/District_of_China "District of China") and 1 [county](/wiki/County_(People%27s_Republic_of_China) "County (People's Republic of China)").

* [Tongguan District](/wiki/Tongguan_District "Tongguan District") (铜官区)
* [Jiao District](/wiki/Jiaoqu,_Tongling "Jiaoqu, Tongling") (郊区)
* [Yi'an District](/wiki/Yi%27an_District "Yi'an District") (义安区)
* [Zongyang County](/wiki/Zongyang_County "Zongyang County") (枞阳县)

In 2012, the provincial government made plans to cede [Zongyang County](/wiki/Zongyang_County "Zongyang County") from [Anqing](/wiki/Anqing "Anqing") City to Tongling City, in order to grant the latter land across the Yangtze.[[14]](#cite_note-14)

## Transport

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Tongling&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Transport")]

As of November 2017, Tongling has [two bridges](/wiki/Tongling_Yangtze_River_Bridge "Tongling Yangtze River Bridge") [across the Yangtze River](/wiki/Yangtze_River_bridges_and_tunnels "Yangtze River bridges and tunnels").

The Tongling Yangtze River Bridge is a double-deck cable-stayed and suspension coordinated system bridge with a total length of 11.88 kilometers and a main span of 988 meters. It has three river crossing functions: expressway, intercity railway, and freight railway.[[15]](#cite_note-15)

### Rail

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Tongling&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Rail")]

Tongling is served by the [Nanjing–Tongling](/wiki/Nanjing%E2%80%93Tongling_Railway "Nanjing–Tongling Railway"), [Tongling–Jiujiang](/wiki/Tongling%E2%80%93Jiujiang_railway "Tongling–Jiujiang railway"), Lujiang-Tongling Railways, [Nanjing–Anqing Intercity Railway](/wiki/Nanjing%E2%80%93Anqing_Intercity_Railway "Nanjing–Anqing Intercity Railway") and [Hefei-Fuzhou High-Speed Railway](/wiki/Hefei-Fuzhou_High-Speed_Railway "Hefei-Fuzhou High-Speed Railway").

## Twin towns

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Tongling&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Twin towns")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/a/a4/Statue_of_Schiller_in_Tianjinghu_scenery_park.jpg/250px-Statue_of_Schiller_in_Tianjinghu_scenery_park.jpg)](/wiki/File:Statue_of_Schiller_in_Tianjinghu_scenery_park.jpg)

Statue of Schiller located at Tongling Tianjinghu park

The contract of town-twinning with the city of [Marbach am Neckar](/wiki/Marbach_am_Neckar "Marbach am Neckar") in [Baden-Württemberg](/wiki/Baden-W%C3%BCrttemberg "Baden-Württemberg"), Germany was established in 1990 and was among the first Sino-German town-twinnings, or sister city relationships. One lady citizen of Marbach am Neckar has been awarded honorary citizenship of Tongling City in 2005, recognizing her relentless effort to keep up the town-twinning. Marbach is the birthplace of the famous German poet [Friedrich Schiller](/wiki/Friedrich_Schiller "Friedrich Schiller") (1759–1805), as a symbol of friendship a statue was erected in local Tianjinhu-Lake-Park.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

Tongling has been twinned with the [Borough of Halton](/wiki/Borough_of_Halton "Borough of Halton") in England since 1997. A Chinese friendship garden was created in the grounds of [Runcorn Town Hall](/wiki/Runcorn_Town_Hall "Runcorn Town Hall") in 2006, including a bronze statue gifted by the twin city of Tongling.[[16]](#cite_note-16)

## Culture

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Tongling&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Culture")]

Tongling has built 5 public libraries, 5 cultural centers, 2 museums, 1 art gallery and 1 theater.[[17]](#cite_note-17)

The Tongling Museum is a special museum with copper culture as its main theme. It fully displays the status that Tongling should have in the history of China's bronze civilization, and displays the development of copper culture and the prospects of the copper industry.[[18]](#cite_note-18)

### Local Products

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Tongling&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Local Products")]

Tongling is famous for a very unique local specialty called white ginger, which has been grown in the area for over 2,000 years. The history of this plant goes back as far as the Spring and Autumn period. It is more than just food; it is a major part of the cultural identity of Tongling.[[19]](#cite_note-:0-19)

The success of Tongling white ginger comes from three traditional farming techniques that are still used today. These include keeping the ginger seeds in special "ginger pavilions" (storage chambers) to help them sprout, planting on high ridges, and using bamboo or thatch to build shelters that provide shade during growth. Local farmers have turned this knowledge into a 24-character rhyming guide: "Deeply dig ridges, cut buds for sowing, erect shade-covering bamboo shelters, harvest by hand-pulling, store seeds in ginger pavilions, and stimulate germination with furnace heat". The step of "digging trenches and raising ridges" is usually done around the Qingming Festival to help the soil drain well.[[19]](#cite_note-:0-19)

Recently, in November 2023, the Food and Agriculture Organization [[19]](#cite_note-:0-19)recognized this system as a Globally Important Agricultural Heritage System.[[20]](#cite_note-20) This shows the global importance of Tongling's farming traditions. Today, this white ginger industry helps local farmers earn more money while keeping ancient Chinese agricultural skills alive for the future.
